violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (deli slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was taken out of service. Per PIC, slicing happens once every couple of days in the morning. Slicer must be broken down and washed rinsed and sanitized every time. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. PIC printed application and will drop it off by end of day. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2025
The hand-washing sink in the bathroom was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (deli slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was taken apart to be sanitized at time of inspection. Slicer must be sanitized after each use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2025

Various foods were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, all food was placed in unit at opening. Food was in unit for less than 4 hours. PIC chose to dispose of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2025

ROP seafood was observed thawing with package in tact. Proper thawing procedures reviewed with PIC. Guidance document provided. ROP fish was 31 degrees. Fish was removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2025

Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. The pizza prep unit was in poor repair. Elevated ambient temperature. Unit taken out of service. Per PIC, unit will be serviced tomorrow. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2025

The floors in the kitchen is constructed of wood (several tiles missing) which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2025
